seoTitle: How to Create Categories in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to create categories in QuickBooks Online step by step so your transactions are organized and your financial reports stay meaningful.
intro: Well-organized categories make the difference between reports that actually tell you something and a jumbled list of transactions. This guide covers how to create new categories in QuickBooks Online and keep them useful.

steps.heading: Open the chart of accounts
steps.body: Go to your chart of accounts, which is where categories for transactions are managed.

steps.heading: Create a new category
steps.body: Select the option to add a new account, choosing the appropriate account and detail type for the category.

steps.heading: Name the category clearly
steps.body: Give the category a name that will make sense in reports months from now, not just in the moment.

steps.heading: Apply the category to transactions
steps.body: Use the new category when categorizing bank feed transactions or entering new expenses and income.

faq.question: How many categories can I create?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online allows a generous number of categories, though it's best to keep the list focused rather than overly granular.

faq.question: Can I create subcategories?
faq.answer: Yes, you can nest categories under a parent category for more detailed organization.

faq.question: What's the difference between a category and a class?
faq.answer: Categories organize transactions by type of income or expense, while classes track transactions across different segments like departments or locations.

faq.question: Can I edit a category name later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can rename a category at any time, and it will update across historical transactions using it.
